symptoms.symptom_name: Profound heavy-limb fatigue crash with orthostatic intolerance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A profound energy crash with orthostatic intolerance on a known ME/CFS background fits a severe flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Active dehydration / inability to self-hydrate while alone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to reach fluids while isolated creates a genuine dehydration and pre-renal risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever/urinary or localising infective symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile with no localising symptoms lowers occult-infection probability, though bloods/urine would confirm.
No melaena/bleeding lowers acute haemorrhage probability, though FBC is needed.
Absence of fatiguable bulbar/ocular/respiratory weakness argues against myasthenia; generalised lethargy differs.
A consistent prior pattern supports a flare over a new organic event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic tachycardia on sitting, mildly dry mucosa, benign exams (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthostatic tachycardia with benign exams fits dysautonomia and mild volume depletion.
Established ME/CFS is the substrate for the crash.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A severe stereotyped ME/CFS crash with orthostatic intolerance, screened negative for infection/anaemia/myasthenia, in an isolated patient who cannot self-hydrate, needs urgent in-home support for fluids and safety rather than a harmful ED transfer — with clear 000 triggers.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To provide fluids and assess hydration/safety with minimal exertion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods (FBC, U&E, CRP, ± urinalysis) via a low-exertion pathway
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude anaemia, infection and electrolyte/renal derangement if concern persists.
Not indicated for a stereotyped crash without red flags; the trip may harm.
